text: Abstract Recently, deep learning (DL) methods have received great attention in the medical image analysis in the field of medical image analysis within medical image analysis in medical image analysis within medical image analysis medical image analysis in healthcare. Medical Image Analysis by Deep Learning in Healthcare: An Exhaustive Survey While DL methods dominate almost every field of studies, deep learning-based methods for medical image analysis in the domain of medical healthcare have revolutionized the manner in which clinical imaging data can be processed in real time by which huge numbers of complex images can be processed by gaining insightful information from these data and thus improving health and productivity in healthcare sector. This surveys investigated latest advances of deep learning methods and applications in medical healthcare studies focusing specifically on deep learning-based approaches in the field of medical image analysis in the domain of healthcare and classified the surveyed literatures into the five based on methods used for deep learning approaches and evaluated based on some criterion. Various deep learning models have their unique unique unique structures such as the Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and Long Short-term Memory (LSTM) models, and their hybrid or coupled models etc. That’s why a detailed information with respective to respective respective structures and their applications using methods, as well as their pros, cons and approaches employed in them and the environments and datasets or platforms or systems used are explained in the surveys and that deep learning models used are identified and the frequently programming language (Python) used in this studies, respectively as well as the publication year of the most studies are evaluated. More over, various future research studies relating to applications of deep learning methods in image analysis in medical healthcare can be thus, accordingly highly promoted through the acquired valuable insights. The evaluation criterion consists of a variety of indices such as, Accuracy, and precision etc., Sensitivity, Specificity and F-Score and Robustness, time complexity etc. and generalizability.
